Bug / Task Description: The tar layout computes the number of fixed-size data blocks for a record by truncating instead of rounding up, so any record whose size is not an exact multiple of the block size is allotted one block too few (and a small non-empty record can be given zero blocks). The block count must round up to cover every byte. Fix the block-count computation to use ceiling division so partial trailing data still gets a full block.
Commit Subject: fix(tar): round data block count up for partial blocks
